conventions.testing: Unit tests in modules, integration tests in tests/ directory, AAA pattern
conventions.error_handling: anyhow for apps, thiserror for libs

patterns: LLM-based validation for false positive reduction

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation

constraints: Must handle multiple languages (Rust, Python, JS)
Binary size increase from grammar crates
test_coverage: High coverage for single-line regex patterns; explicit negative test for multi-line vulnerabilities.

library_findings.notes: Specific grammar for Rust AST parsing

approaches.name: Hybrid Regex-AST Scanner
approaches.description: Keep regex for simple patterns (secrets, weak crypto) and add Tree-sitter for complex multi-line patterns (SQLi, Command Injection).

approaches.name: Full Tree-sitter Migration
approaches.description: Convert all 14 regex patterns to Tree-sitter queries and use AST for all security scanning.
cons: High upfront effort to rewrite all patterns
Potential performance hit for simple checks

recommendation: Hybrid approach. Start by implementing Tree-sitter for the known multi-line SQL injection limitation while keeping regex for high-confidence single-line patterns like hardcoded secrets.
